summary: Architect §8 on the shared plan resolves the one decision the task_planner deferred (plan §1 + appendix forest-note): the code-integration mechanism and siting for the R8 determinism-boundary code and the R10 router in the gh-aw single-session model. Decision: TS CLI tools under workflows/review/lib/ (co-located inside the self-contained workflows/review/ package), tested with the existing vitest/@swc-node/register, invoked by the orchestrator via bash (node -r @swc-node/register) over JSON in /tmp/gh-aw/review/; orchestrator stays the single session and sole safe-output caller. Three integration surfaces named (in-session CLIs / standalone scheduled workflows / no-post eval harness); in-session data-flow contract, no-post mechanism, router's single model touch, and boundary-enforcement review wiring fixed. Slice DAG unchanged and convergent with the task_planner's; the three operator-named edges hold with no re-ordering.

integration_decision.mechanism: TypeScript CLI tools invoked by the orchestrator via bash (node -r @swc-node/register), exchanging JSON on disk under the existing /tmp/gh-aw/review/ scratch dir; the orchestrator remains the single agentic session and the sole caller of GitHub and safe outputs.
integration_decision.toolchain: Reuse existing tooling: vitest (root config discovers **/*.test.ts via @swc-node/register, so .ts runs without a separate build); the orchestrator runs the CLIs via node -r @swc-node/register (or a pre-bundled entry).
integration_decision.siting: workflows/review/lib/ (support code) + workflows/review/eval/ (harness), co-located inside the self-contained workflows/review/ package (its own package.json). Endorses the task_planner's indicative paths name-for-name; rejects actions/ siting because actions/* is for reusable cross-workflow composite actions and this code is review-internal.
rejected_alternatives: Multi-job split (router/verdict as separate GH Actions jobs) — fights gh-aw's single-session model and forces cross-job state hand-off.
Siting under actions/ — actions/* is for reusable composite actions shared across workflows; review-internal code belongs inside the workflows/review/ package (cohesion).
Bespoke runtime/build system — rejected in favor of reusing vitest + @swc-node/register the repo already has (reuse over rebuild).

integration_surfaces.name: in-session CLIs (per review run)
integration_surfaces.note: Only surface that touches a live PR; orchestrator invokes via bash over /tmp/gh-aw/review/*.json.

integration_surfaces.name: standalone scheduled/polling workflows (no per-PR session)
integration_surfaces.note: R4's both-consumer-repos guarantee (interface §4.3) realized as a config-parameterized standalone workflow, not a consumer commit.

integration_surfaces.name: CI / scheduled no-post eval harness
integration_surfaces.note: Drives the same code pipeline (and scored model sub-agents) with safe-outputs stubbed; one dataset format/runner/no-post mode.

in_session_dataflow: orchestrator stages full.diff + files.json -> router CLI -> routing.json (lensesToSpawn, teams, perFileTier, runBudget); Step 3 dispatch consumes it; reviewer-mapper dispatch removed
each sub-agent writes out/<agent>.json (#194 flow) -> schema-validate against schema.ts; malformed => dimension treated as unavailable, feeding the R2 missing-dimension gate (not silently dropped)
validated findings + posted-comment labels -> verdict CLI -> verdict.json {event: APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|HOLD_FOR_HUMAN, reasons[]}; consumes #194's mechanical label rule, does not re-implement it
findings + verdict -> render CLI -> comment/review bodies; orchestrator posts via existing safe outputs (submit-pull-request-review, add-comment); code never posts

design_constraints.no_post_run_mode: safe-output calls are the only side-effecting boundary; no-post mode swaps that one boundary for a capture sink (mechanism behind R5/R6/R11 / task-9-2 / task-10-4).
design_constraints.router_model_touch: router core is pure deterministic TS; the only judgment (diff-direction-dependent risk tiers) is emitted as a bounded question, resolved by the orchestrator's single small-model call, and fed back on a second router invocation — the deterministic core never calls a model.
design_constraints.boundary_enforcement: physical CLI/prose split makes the R8 converse tripwire mechanically checkable: any diff adding prose synthesis to workflows/review/lib/* or scoring beyond declared severity/confidence fields is scope creep to reject in review.
design_constraints.trigger_override_integrity: adding Surface-A bash invocations to the orchestrator prompt touches no on:/label frontmatter, so webapp manual /review and frontend automatic mode (interface §4.6) are preserved by construction.

dependency_edges_honored: router (S3) before the lenses that consume its routing (S7)
finding schema (S1) before the computed verdict (S2)
smoke set (S9) before the wave-2 rebalance (S10)
dag_convergence: The architect integration design and the task_planner slice DAG were reached independently and converge; §8 adds the how-code-is-invoked layer with no re-ordering of §3 and no change to the task_planner's roles/tasks/ordering.
reuse_over_rebuild: existing vitest + @swc-node/register (runs .ts without a separate build; no new build config)
existing workflows/review/ self-contained package (its own package.json) as the code home
existing safe outputs (submit-pull-request-review, add-comment) — code never posts
single shared finding-schema.ts imported by all three surfaces so findings are one type everywhere
no_decisions_rationale: No HITL decision is registered at plan time. The four proposal open questions were resolved by the operator at the refine gate and are non-blocking; the seven 2026-07-02 directions resolved this phase's structural judgment calls. The §8 integration decision is a pure technical-design choice (CLI-over-bash vs multi-job vs bespoke runtime; actions/review/ vs workflows/review/lib/ siting) on reuse/single-session grounds, changing no scope and no ordering — no operator judgment required. The pre-declared security-lens-split trigger (plan §6/task-7-1) and the residual blocking-verdict threshold remain HITL-if-a-build-choice-depends per directive 7; neither is live now.
